reasoning_content: We need to check if 359 is a prime number. A quick sanity check: test divisibility by small primes. 359 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 3+5+9=17, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*51=357, remainder 2, so not divisible. 11: 11*32=352, remainder 7; 11*33=363, too high. 13: 13*27=351, remainder 8; 13*28=364. 17: 17*21=357, remainder 2; 17*22=374. 19: 19*18=342, remainder 17; 19*19=361, too high. 23: 23*15=345, remainder 14; 23*16=368. 29: 29*12=348, remainder 11; 29*13=377. 31: 31*11=341, remainder 18; 31*12=372. sqrt(359) is about 18.95, so we only need to test primes up to 17. We've tested 2,3,5,7,11,13,17. None divide 359. So 359 is prime. Quick sanity check: 359 is indeed a prime number.
